LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION CONTINUED FROM NOV-03-2009 TO LAW AND MOTION AT NOV-10-2009, 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302. (THIS MATTER WAS ADDED TO CALENDAR ON 11/3/09 BECAUSE PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL FAILED TO FILE HIS EX PARTE ORDER WHEN HE APPEARED AT THE EX PARTE CALENDAR THE PRIOR WEEK.) HEARING HELD. MATTER CONTINUED TO NOVEMBER 10, 2009 FOR HEARING. DEFENSE COUNSEL FOR UNITEDLAYER, LLC WILL FILE HIS CORRECTED OPPOSITION TODAY WITH THE CLERK'S OFFICE; REPLY IS DUE 11/5/09. COURTESY COPIES OF ALL FILINGS ARE TO BE SENT DIRECTLY TO DEPT. 302. ANY AND ALL PREVIOUS ORDERS REMAIN IN EFFECT. MR. LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION DENIED. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO SHOW A LIKELIHOOD OF SUCCESS ON THE MERITS. PLAINTIFF IS THE PARTY IN BREACH, AND IS IN DEFAULT ON SEPTEMBER AND OCTOBER PAYMENTS. AGREEMENT ENTITLES DEFENDANT TO OBTAIN A LIEN ON THE PROPERTY. ALTHOUGH PLAINTIFF MAY NOT BE THE PROPER PARTY IN THIS ACTION, AND "LIVE VIDEO" IS NOT REGISTERED IN CALIFORNIA THE COURT DOES NOT MAKE A DETERMINATION REGARDING THIS ISSUE IN THIS DECISION. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Sep-13-2010 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: Notice having been given and there being no appearance nor an objection filed. The court orders case dismissed pursuant to California Rules of Court Section 3.1385. Proceedings not reported. (212)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-27-2010 Continued To Jul-06-10 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 212

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-08-2010 Set For Order To Show Cause On May-03-10 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 212

MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Jan-05-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Kassra P. Nassiri Esq. & Andrew R. Kislik, Esq. Motion to be relieved as counsel for PLAINTIFF LIVEUNIVERSE, INC. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Granted. No opposition filed. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

Plaintiff'S Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Nov-10-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Plaintiff's Order to show cause re: preliminary injunction. Appearances: Andrew R. Kislik, Esq. & Kassra Powell Nassiri, Esq. of Nassiri & Jung LLP (415) 373-5699 appearing for Plaintiff and Moving party; Paul S. Sigelman, Esq. of Sigelman Law Firm (310) 278-8011 appearing for Defendant United Layer, Inc. and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for preliminary injunction denied. Plaintiff fails to show a likelihood of success on the merits. Plaintiff is the party in breach, and is in default on September and October payments. Agreement entitles defendant to obtain a lien on the property. Although plaintiff may not be the proper party in this action, and "Live Video" is not registered in California the court does not make a determination regarding this issue in this decision. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

Plaintiff'S Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Nov-03-2009 Continued To Nov-10-09 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: Plaintiff's Order to Show Cause re: Preliminary Injunction. Kassra Powell Nassiri, Esq. of Nassiri & Jung LLP (415) 373-5699 appearing for Plaintiff and moving party LiveUniverse; Paul S. Sigelman, Esq. of Sigelman Law Firm (310) 278-8011 appearing thru Courtcall for Defendant United layer LLC. (This matter was added to Calendar on 11/3/09 because plaintiff's counsel failed to file his ex parte Order when he appeared at the ex parte calendar the prior week.) Hearing held. Ruling: Matter continued to November 10, 2009 for hearing. Defense counsel for UnitedLayer, LLC will file his corrected opposition today with the Clerk's Office; reply is due 11/5/09. Courtesy copies of all filings are to be sent directly to Dept. 302. Any and all previous orders remain in effect. Mr. Nassiri is to prepare an Order for the Court's signature regarding today's hearing. (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
